title: Authentic Thai Tom Kha Gai
ingredients: 3 slices galangal, around 1.5 inches wide and slightly larger than the thickness of a quarter. Smash lightly to release oils.
3 lemongrass stalks; use only the rounded light green part at the bottom. Smash lightly to release oils.
1 pound Boneless Skinless Chicken Breasts, cut into bite-sized pieces
2 cups of thinly sliced mushrooms (i.e., button, shitake, Enoki, or oyster mushroom)
3-4 tablespoons Fish Sauce. Adjust as needed as each brand of fish sauce has a different saltiness level.
¼ cup Cilantro stems and leafy parts chopped
instructions: In a large saucepan or Dutch oven on medium heat, add stock, galangal, lemongrass, and kaffir lime leaves. Bring to a gentle boil. (You are making a quick stock here for the soup base.)
Add chicken and mushroom. Lower heat to medium-high. Cook until the chicken is done (anywhere from 7-8 minutes, depending on your stovetop).
Add the entire can of coconut milk (I used Aroy-D for this recipe). Stir together and cook for 2-3 minutes.
Add the cilantro stems and fish sauce, stir, and cook for two more minutes. Taste and adjust the flavors with more fish sauce, lime juice, or sugar. Turn the heat off and serve.
Add lime juice and Thai chilis (if using) and stir in one last time. Turn the heat off and remove the saucepan from the stovetop.
Let cool for a few minutes and portion out in soup bowls for serving. That's it! Simple, easy, healthy, creamy, light, and super delicious!

title: Beef Stew
ingredients: 2 1/2 pounds chuck beef cut into 1-inch cubes
1 cup good quality dry Red Wine, such as Pinot Noir, Burgundy or. Cote du Rhone
1 teaspoon chopped fresh Rosemary, or ½ teaspoon Dried Rosemary
1 teaspoon fresh Thyme leaves, or ½ teaspoon dried
4 large Carrots peeled, sliced diagonally into chunks
1/2 pound mini gold or fingerling Potatoes, cut in small chunks
8 ounces frozen whole pearl onions (optional or to use fresh pearl onions, see note below)
instructions: Add bacon to a large Dutch oven or stock pot. Turn heat to medium-low and cook, turning flipping every so often, until browned. Remove to a paper towel lined plate and then chop into small pieces.
Pat beef cubes dry with paper towels and add to a ziplock bag with flour, salt and pepper. Toss well to coat.

Heat the pan with bacon grease over medium-high heat. Once hot, add the beef in 2-3 batches (so not to overcrowd the pan), adding a little oil as needed, searing for a few minutes on each side, until browned. Remove meat to a plate and set aside. Reduce heat to medium.

Add onion and cook for 2-3 minutes. Add garlic and cook for 30 seconds. Add vinegar and wine to the pan, cooking over medium-high heat, and scraping up any browned bits from the bottom of the pan. Simmer for 10 minutes.
Add beef broth, bullion, tomato paste, bay leaves, thyme and rosemary. Season with a little more salt and pepper. Add beef and bacon back to the pot. Bring to a boil then reduce heat to a simmer.
Cover and cook for 1 ½ hours or until beef is tender.
Meanwhile, chop the potatoes, carrots, and slice the mushrooms. Set aside.

Cook mushrooms (if using): Add 2 tablespoons butter and 1 tablespoon olive oil to a saute pan over medium high heat. Add mushrooms to the pan in a single layer. Cook for several minutes (without touching). Once golden on the bottom, flip to the other side and cook for a few more minutes.
Add mushrooms, potatoes, carrots and frozen pearl onions to the soup pot and cook for 15-20 more minutes, until carrot and potatoes are tender.
Taste broth. Season as needed with additional salt, pepper, bullion, rosemary, thyme, or some garlic powder. Add a sprinkle of crushed red pepper, if desired.
Ladle some of the hot broth into a bowl and whisk the flour into it, until smooth. Add to pot and cook for 2-5 minutes, until broth has slightly thickened.
Garnish with fresh parsley. Serve with artisan bread.

title: Chinese Braised Beef Shank
instructions: Trim the excess fat off the beef and cut the shank into two to three 1 lb. chunks. Boil a pot of water and boil the beef for about 8 minutes. Drain and rinse the meat.
Put the boiled beef shanks in a small, deep saucepan over medium-low heat and add the wine, 2 kinds of soy sauce, star anise, chili, rock sugar, and cinnamon.
Make sure the meat is all coated in the sauce, rolling it around in the pot for a few minutes.

Add enough water to submerge the meat about 2/3 of the way (you may need more or less depending on the size and shape of your pot). Turn the heat to medium-high, cover the pot, and bring it to a boil. Once it's boiling, lower the heat and simmer, covered, for 2 hours. Every 30 minutes or so, uncover the pot and flip over the meat. This ensures that the marinade distributes evenly.
Turn off the heat and allow the meat to sit in the covered pot for about 2 hours, turning once again halfway through the cooling process.
Then allow the meat to cool completely in the refrigerator, preferably overnight. Take it out of the pot, slice it thinly, and garnish with sesame seeds and scallions.

title: Cornbread
instructions: Preheat oven to 400°F. Grease an 8-inch cast iron skillet or square baking pan with butter.
In a large bowl, whisk together the cornmeal, flour, sugar, baking powder, and salt.
In a separate bowl, whisk together the milk, melted butter, egg, and honey.
Pour the wet ingredients into the dry ingredients and stir until just combined — do not overmix. A few small lumps are fine.
Pour the batter into the prepared skillet. Bake for 20-25 minutes until the top is golden and a toothpick inserted in the center comes out clean.
Let cool 5 minutes before cutting into wedges. Serve warm with butter and honey.

title: Crying Tiger Thai Grilled Beef
ingredients: 1.2-1.5 pounds grilling steak (rib eye, Top Sirloin Steak, flank, or any favorite grilling cut)
1 tablespoon thin Low-Sodium Soy Sauce or regular Low-Sodium Soy Sauce
2 tablespoons tamarind concentrate (optional, for extra sourness)
1-3 tablespoons Crushed Red Pepper Flakes (adjust to taste)
2 tablespoons uncooked Jasmine Rice (for toasting into rice powder)
instructions: Wash your meat with cold water, pat dry with paper towel, and place it in a plastic bag or shallow pan.
Mix the oyster sauce, fish sauce, soy sauce, garlic, sugar, and white pepper in a medium bowl until the sugar dissolves.
Coat the meat in the marinade on both sides. If your meat is thick, use a small knife to puncture a few holes to let the sauce seep through.
Pour the sauce over the meat in a ziplock bag or small pan, cover, and marinate in the refrigerator for 2-3 hours (or at least 30 minutes for a quick version, up to overnight).

While the meat marinates, make the Nam Jim Jaew dipping sauce. Heat a dry pan over medium-low heat. Add uncooked rice and toast, stirring often, until the grains turn golden brown and fragrant (about 5 minutes). Remove from heat, let cool completely, then grind into a coarse powder using a spice grinder or mortar and pestle.

Finely chop the green onion, cilantro, and shallot and place them in a small mixing bowl. Warm the honey briefly in the microwave for 15-20 seconds until runny, let cool slightly, then add to the herbs and stir to combine.

Add fish sauce, fresh lime juice, water, tamarind concentrate (if using), and chili flakes to the herb mixture. Stir until evenly mixed. Taste and adjust with more lime juice, fish sauce, or water as needed. Stir in the toasted rice powder until fully incorporated. Set aside at room temperature.

Start the grill and cook the meat on medium-high heat. Cover the grill and cook until the meat is done to your preference — about 4-5 minutes per side for medium-rare (130°F internal), or 5-6 minutes per side for medium (140°F internal).
Remove from the grill and let the meat rest for 10-15 minutes on a cutting board before cutting to allow the juices to redistribute.

Cut the meat against the grain into thin strips. Serve with the Nam Jim Jaew dipping sauce, sticky rice, and fresh vegetables such as cabbage wedges, cucumber slices, and fresh herbs like Thai basil and mint.

title: Easy Slow Cooker White Chicken Chili
ingredients: 2 15oz. cans cannellini beans, drained and rinsed
1 15oz. can Pinto Beans, drained and rinsed
¼ teaspoon Freshly Ground Black Pepper
instructions: Dice the onion and mince the garlic. Slice the jalapeńo lengthwise, scrape out the seeds with a spoon, then dice. Place the onion, garlic, jalapeño, and chicken breast in the bottom of a slow cooker.
Drain and rinse the cannellini beans and pinto beans, then add them to the slow cooker, along with the cumin, oregano, cayenne, and freshly ground black pepper. Pour the salsa over top.
Pour two cups of chicken broth over the contents in the cooker and then give everything a brief stir. Place the lid on the slow cooker and cook on high for four hours, or on low for eight hours.

After four hours on high or eight hours on low, remove the crockpot lid and carefully use two forks to shred the chicken meat (you can also remove it, shred it on a cutting board, and then return the meat to the slow cooker). Stir the chili, slightly mashing the beans with the back of the spoon as you stir. The mashed beans will help thicken the chili.
Serve the chili topped with your favorite ingredients, including shredded cheese, fresh cilantro, diced avocado, freshly squeezed lime, or tortilla chips (all optional).

title: French Onion Soup
ingredients: 1 1/2 pounds White Onion (about 3 large onions), thinly sliced
1 piece Celery, just a small chunk, not the whole rib
instructions: Caramelize onions: Add butter and onions to a large pot over medium heat. Season with a little salt and pepper and cook until softened. Add sugar and continue to gently stir until onions become golden brown and caramelized, about 25 minutes. Careful to not let them burn! Maintain a medium to medium-low heat.
Add the flour and cook for 3 more minutes. Add beef stock, wine, thyme, celery, and bay leaves. Cook partially covered for 30 minutes.
Preheat oven to 350 degrees F.

Toast bread: Arrange bread on a baking sheet and brush with melted butter on both sides. Bake for 15 minutes, flipping to the other side half way through. Remove from oven and rub a cut clove of garlic onto each piece of bread.

Broil: Turn oven to High broil. Remove bay leaves and the piece of celery from the soup and ladle soup into 4 oven proof bowls. Place the toasted bread on top of each bowl. Depending on the size of your bowls you may need two slices of bread. Sprinkle the tops generously with both types of cheese. Broil until the cheese is browned and bubbling.

title: Pickled Red Onions
ingredients: 1 large Red Onion, thinly sliced into rings
instructions: Thinly slice the red onion into rings or half-moons. Place in a clean mason jar or heat-proof container.
In a small bowl, combine the vinegar, warm water, sugar, and salt. Stir until the sugar and salt are fully dissolved.
Pour the pickling liquid over the sliced onions, making sure they are fully submerged. Add peppercorns and bay leaf if using.
Let sit at room temperature for at least 30 minutes. For best flavor, refrigerate for 1 hour before serving. Store in the fridge for up to 3 weeks.

title: Pulled BBQ Chicken Sandwiches with Creamy Classic Coleslaw
instructions: Begin by preparing the coleslaw first, as it needs time to chill: In a small bowl, whisk together 1/3 cup mayonnaise, 1 tablespoon apple cider vinegar, 1 tablespoon sugar, 1/2 teaspoon celery seed, salt, and pepper until smooth.

In a large bowl, combine 3 cups shredded cabbage and 1 cup shredded carrots. Pour the dressing over the cabbage mixture and toss until evenly coated. Cover and refrigerate for at least 30 minutes (or while the chicken cooks) to allow the flavors to meld together.

**For the BBQ Pulled Chicken:** In the slow cooker, combine 1 cup BBQ sauce, 1/2 cup chicken broth, 2 tablespoons brown sugar, 1 tablespoon apple cider vinegar, 1 teaspoon smoked paprika, 1/2 teaspoon garlic powder, and 1/2 teaspoon onion powder. Stir well to combine all sauce ingredients.

Place 3 pounds boneless skinless chicken breasts in the slow cooker and turn to coat evenly with the sauce. Cover and cook on low for 6-8 hours or high for 3-4 hours until the chicken is tender and easy to shred.

When the chicken is cooked through and tender, remove it from the slow cooker and shred it using two forks. Return the shredded chicken to the slow cooker and mix it into the sauce. Let it warm in the sauce for an additional 10 minutes to absorb the flavors.
While the chicken is absorbing the sauce in its final minutes, check the coleslaw and adjust seasoning if needed. Remove it from the refrigerator to take the chill off slightly.
Toast the sandwich buns if desired for 1-2 minutes until lightly golden.

**To Serve:** Assemble your sandwiches by placing a generous amount of the BBQ pulled chicken on the bottom half of each sandwich bun. Top with a portion of the prepared coleslaw and add pickle slices if desired. Cover with the top half of the bun and serve immediately.

title: Quick & Authentic Chicken Tikka Masala with Basmati Rice & Naan
ingredients: 1 tablespoon Fresh Grated Ginger, minced (or 1 teaspoon ginger paste)
1 tablespoon oil (e.g., vegetable or avocado oil)
instructions: Preheat your oven to 400°F for the naan bread.
Cut 1 lb boneless skinless chicken breasts into 1-inch pieces and season lightly with salt and pepper.
Slice 1 small yellow onion into medium-sized pieces.
Remove the package of frozen Basmati rice from the freezer to slightly thaw while preparing the chicken.
Heat 1 tablespoon of oil in a large skillet over medium-high heat.
Add the sliced onion and cook for 2–3 minutes, or until it starts to soften.
Stir in 2 cloves minced garlic and 1 tablespoon minced ginger (or 1 teaspoon ginger paste), cooking for about 1 minute—just until fragrant (avoid burning).
Add the seasoned chicken pieces and sauté for another 3–4 minutes, browning them on all sides.

Pour the Tikka Masala sauce over the chicken, stir well to coat, then reduce the heat to medium-low. Cover and simmer for about 8–10 minutes, or until the chicken is fully cooked (internal temp 165°F/74°C). If the sauce appears too thick, add a small splash of water.
While the chicken is simmering, remove frozen naan bread from packaging and place it in the preheated oven for 4-5 minutes until warm and soft.
For the Rice: Transfer the frozen Basmati rice to a microwave-safe bowl, cover, and heat according to package instructions (usually 3-4 minutes).
For Enhanced Naan (optional): Mix 1-2 tablespoons melted butter with 1 clove minced garlic. When the naan is warm, brush with the garlic butter mixture and sprinkle with chopped cilantro.
Fluff the rice with a fork before serving.
Serve the chicken tikka masala over the basmati rice with warm naan bread on the side.

title: Raita (Yogurt Cucumber Sauce)
ingredients: 1 cup Plain Greek Yogurt (full fat for best results)
1 tablespoon Fresh Mint Leaves, finely chopped
instructions: Dice the cucumber finely. If using a regular cucumber, peel and remove seeds first. English cucumber can be used with skin on.
In a medium bowl, combine the yogurt, diced cucumber, fresh mint, cumin, salt, and cayenne pepper.
Stir gently until well combined. Add lemon juice and stir once more.
Cover and refrigerate for at least 15 minutes to let flavors meld. Serve cold alongside curries, tikka masala, or any spicy Indian dish.

title: Red Wine-Braised Short Ribs with Buttery Garlic Mashed Potatoes
ingredients: 1 cup Red Wine (such as Cote du Rhone or Pinot Noir)
5 medium Russet or Yukon Gold potatoes
1/2 - 3/4 cup milk (2%, whole, or cream are all great)
instructions: Preheat the oven to 350 degrees F.
Season 8 whole beef short ribs generously with salt and pepper, then coat all sides with 1/4 cup all-purpose flour.
Heat 1 tablespoon of olive oil in a large Dutch oven over medium-high heat.
Add 1 diced yellow onion and 3 chopped carrots, cooking for a few minutes while stirring constantly.
Remove the vegetables to a plate and set aside.
Add the remaining 2 tablespoons of olive oil to the pot and increase heat to high.
Brown the ribs on all sides, about 45 seconds per side, then remove to a plate.
Pour 1 cup of red wine into the hot pot to deglaze, scraping the bottom to release any browned bits of flavor.
Bring the wine to a boil and cook for 2 minutes to reduce slightly.
Add 2 1/2 cups beef broth, salt, pepper, and 2 tablespoons of tomato paste, stirring to combine.
Taste the broth and adjust seasonings as needed.
Return the ribs and vegetables to the pot, then add 2 whole sprigs of thyme and 2 whole sprigs of rosemary.
Cover with the lid and transfer to the preheated oven.
Cook at 350 degrees F for 2 hours. **For the Mashed Potatoes:**
While the short ribs are cooking, cut 5 medium potatoes in half and place in a large pot with about 4 inches of water.
Cover and cook the potatoes until fork tender, about 15-20 minutes, timing this to finish about 45 minutes before the short ribs are done.
Drain water and leave potatoes in the pot, covered for 5 minutes to dry out.
Add 1/3 cup butter, 1 teaspoon chicken bouillon (if using), 1/2 cup milk, 1/4 cup sour cream (if using), salt, and pepper to the pot.
Mash with a potato masher until creamy and smooth, adding more milk (up to 3/4 cup total) if needed.
Taste and adjust seasoning with additional salt and pepper if needed.
Cover the mashed potatoes to keep warm while the short ribs finish cooking. **Back to the Short Ribs:**
After the initial 2 hours of cooking, reduce oven heat to 325 degrees F and cook the short ribs for an additional 30 to 45 minutes until they are very tender, nearly falling off the bone.
Remove from the oven and let rest for 20 minutes with the lid on.
Skim off any fat that has risen to the top of the liquid.
Serve the ribs over the mashed potatoes with a spoonful of the rich gravy from the pot.

title: Slow Cooker Chicken Tikka Masala
ingredients: 1/4 of a large Yellow Onion, diced
1/4 fresh Jalapeño pepper, optional, seeds removed, finely chopped
4 cups Hot cooked Basmati Rice, for serving
instructions: Mix sauce ingredients together in a bowl.
Place chicken thighs in slow cooker and season with salt and pepper. Pour sauce over chicken. Cook on LOW for 6-7 hours.
Shred chicken (discard any bones). Stir in coconut milk. Add cilantro. Serve over hot cooked basmati rice.

title: Slow Cooker Chicken Tikka Masala
ingredients: 1/4 of a large Yellow Onion, diced
instructions: Make the sauce: In a medium bowl, whisk together 2 tablespoons olive oil, 2 cloves minced garlic, 1 tablespoon minced fresh ginger, 1/4 diced yellow onion, 3 teaspoons garam masala, 1/2 teaspoon ground cumin, and 1/4 teaspoon cayenne pepper. Stir in 2 cans (8 oz each) tomato sauce and 1 can (15 oz) diced tomatoes until well combined.
Prep the chicken: Place 2 pounds chicken thighs in the slow cooker in a single layer. Season with 1/2 teaspoon salt and 1/4 teaspoon black pepper.
Add the sauce: Pour the prepared sauce evenly over the chicken thighs, making sure they are well coated. Cover the slow cooker with the lid.
Slow cook: Cook on LOW for 6-7 hours, or until the chicken is very tender and falls apart easily. Do not cook on HIGH as the sauce may scorch.

Shred and finish: Remove the chicken thighs and shred with two forks, discarding any bones. Return the shredded chicken to the slow cooker. Stir in 1 cup coconut milk until the sauce is creamy and well combined. Let warm through for 5 minutes.
Serve: Ladle the tikka masala over 4 cups hot cooked basmati rice. Garnish with 1/4 cup fresh chopped cilantro. Serve with warm naan bread on the side.

title: Slow Cooker Coconut Curry Lentils
ingredients: 1/2 bunch Fresh Cilantro or Green Onions
instructions: Dice the onion and mince the garlic. Peel the sweet potato and carrots. Dice the sweet potato (1/4-1/2 inch cubes) and slice the carrots.

Add the onion, garlic, sweet potato, carrots, lentils, curry powder, cloves, diced tomatoes, tomato sauce, and vegetable broth to the slow cooker. Stir to combine. Place the lid on the slow cooker and cook on high for 4 hours or low for 7-8 hours. Once cooked, the lentils should be tender and most of the liquid should be absorbed.

Stir the can of coconut milk into the lentils. Taste and adjust the salt or other spices as needed (the amount of salt needed will depend on the type of broth used and the salt content of the canned tomatoes).
To serve, add 1 cup cooked rice to a bowl followed by 1 cup of the lentil mixture. Top with finely diced red onion and fresh cilantro.

title: Smoky Spanish Chorizo & Lentil Stew with Crisp Romaine Salad
ingredients: 2 small Yukon Gold Potatoes, cut into ½ inch pieces
1/2 cup fresh chopped Flat-Leaf Parsley, to garnish
instructions: Begin by preparing all your vegetables: finely dice 1 medium yellow onion, dice 1 red bell pepper, thinly slice 1 carrot for the stew, slice 2 celery stalks, roughly mince 4 cloves of garlic, and cut 2 small Yukon Gold potatoes into ½ inch pieces.
Slice the 8oz Spanish chorizo into ¼ inch thick rounds.
Rinse 1 cup of dry brown lentils under cold water and set aside.
Wash 1 head of romaine lettuce and break into bite-sized pieces.
Chop 2 Roma tomatoes into small chunks.
Drain the small can of sliced olives. ## For the Main Dish: Chorizo Lentil Stew
Heat a large pot on medium heat. Add the chorizo slices in a single layer and cook for 1 minute on each side to render some fat. Transfer to a paper towel-lined plate.
Add 1 tablespoon of olive oil to the pot. Add all the prepared vegetables (onion, red bell pepper, carrot, and celery - except the potatoes) and sauté for 5 minutes.
Add the 4 cloves of minced garlic, 1 teaspoon smoked paprika, and ½ teaspoon dried oregano and stir to combine.
Add ½ cup tomato sauce and cook for 2-3 minutes.
Add the rinsed lentils, potato pieces, cooked chorizo, 6 cups vegetable broth, 1 tablespoon red wine vinegar, and 2 bay leaves.
Bring the stew to a boil, then reduce heat to low, cover the pot, and simmer for 20-30 minutes or until the lentils are tender. ## For the Side Salad
While the stew is simmering, in a large bowl, combine the prepared romaine lettuce, chopped tomatoes, drained olives, and shredded carrots.
Just before serving, drizzle the salad with oil and vinegar dressing to taste.
Add a pinch of salt and toss to combine. ## Final Steps
When the lentils are tender, taste the stew and season with salt and pepper as needed.
Remove the bay leaves from the stew.
Serve the hot chorizo lentil stew in bowls garnished with some of the ½ cup chopped flat-leaf parsley.
Serve the tossed salad immediately alongside the stew for a refreshing contrast.

title: Spicy Penne Arrabbiata with Crisp Garden Side Salad
ingredients: 1 pound penne rigate (most packages are 1 pound)
1 28-ounce can of whole peeled tomatoes (or 1 1/2 cups fresh chopped tomatoes)
instructions: Begin by preparing the ingredients: Slice 1 cup cherry tomatoes in half, slice 1/2 cup cucumbers, thinly slice 1/4 cup red onions, mince 3 cloves of garlic, chop 6 fresh basil leaves and 1/3 cup flat-leaf parsley.
Fill a large pot with water for the pasta and bring to a boil. This will take several minutes, so you can work on other components while waiting.

If using 1/2 pound ground beef, heat a large pan over medium heat and cook the beef, breaking it into small pieces as it browns. Season with salt and freshly ground black pepper. Once cooked, drain excess fat and set aside.

In the same pan (or a clean one if not using beef), heat 3 tablespoons olive oil over medium heat. Add the 3 cloves minced garlic and 1/4 teaspoon crushed red pepper flakes (if using). Cook for about 30 seconds, stirring constantly, until fragrant.
Add 1 28-ounce can of whole peeled tomatoes and 2 tablespoons tomato paste to the pan. Crush the tomatoes with a spoon and mix with the other ingredients.
Bring the sauce to a low simmer. If using ground beef, add it back to the sauce. Simmer for 5-10 minutes to develop flavor.
When the pot of water is boiling, add salt and cook 1 pound penne rigate according to package instructions, usually 8-10 minutes for al dente pasta.

While the pasta and sauce are cooking, prepare the salad. In a large salad bowl, combine 6 cups mixed greens, 1 cup halved cherry tomatoes, 1/2 cup sliced cucumbers, 1/4 cup thinly sliced red onions, and 1/2 cup shredded carrots.
When the pasta is done cooking, drain it well but do not rinse.
Remove the sauce from heat. Add the chopped 6 fresh basil leaves and the drained pasta to the pan with the sauce. Toss gently to coat the pasta evenly.
For the Salad: Sprinkle the prepared salad with 1/4 cup crumbled queso fresco if desired. Set aside with dressing of choice on the side.
Back to the Main Dish: Transfer the pasta to a serving dish and top with 1/2 cup freshly grated Parmesan cheese and the chopped 1/3 cup parsley.
Serve the pasta hot alongside the garden salad. Just before eating the salad, drizzle with your favorite dressing and toss gently to combine.

title: Stuffed Shells
ingredients: 20 jumbo pasta shells, (cooked according to package directions*)
2 tablespoons chopped Fresh Basil Leaves, (for garnish)
instructions: Preheat oven to 375 degree F. Spray a 9x13-inch baking dish with nonstick cooking spray. Set aside.

Bring a large pot of salted water to a boil. Add pasta shells and cook until al dente, according to package instructions. Don't overcook the shells. Drain, and rinse with run cold water to stop cooking. Set the shells aside.
In a large skillet, heat the olive oil over medium heat. Add the chopped spinach and sauté until wilted. Add the garlic and cook for 1 minute.
In a large bowl, combine ricotta cheese, egg, spinach mixture, 1 cup of the mozzarella cheese, Parmesan cheese, Italian seasoning, red pepper flakes, salt, and pepper.
Spread 1 1/2 cups of the marinara sauce on the bottom of the baking dish.
Use a spoon to fill the pasta shells with the cheese and spinach mixture. Place in the baking dish.
Pour the remaining marinara sauce over the shells and sprinkle with remaining mozzarella cheese.
Cover with foil and bake for 30 minutes. Carefully remove the foil and broil for an additional 2 minutes to lightly brown the cheese.
Remove from the oven and garnish with fresh basil. Serve warm.

title: Turkey Sweet Potato Chili
ingredients: 1 Red Bell Pepper, (cored, seeded, and small-diced)
Kosher Salt and Black Pepper, (to taste)
1 (15 oz) can Kidney Beans, rinsed and drained (can use Black Beans)
1 large Sweet Potatoes, (peeled and diced into 1/2-inch cubes (about 2 cups))
Optional toppings: Shredded Cheese, thinly sliced Green Onions, Sour Cream or plain Greek Yogurt, jalapeño slices, Avocado
instructions: Heat the olive oil in a large pot or Dutch oven over medium heat. Add the onion and red bell pepper and cook, stirring occasionally, until softened, about 4 to 5 minutes. Add the garlic and cook for 1 minute, just until fragrant.
Add the ground turkey and cook, breaking it up with a wooden spoon, until browned and cooked through, about 5 to 7 minutes. Season lightly with salt and pepper.
Stir in 2 tablespoons of the chili powder, cumin, smoked paprika, and oregano. Cook for 1 minute to toast the spices.
Add the fire-roasted tomatoes (with their juices), beans, sweet potato, and broth. Stir to combine.

Bring the chili to a boil, then reduce the heat to low, cover, and simmer for 30 minutes, stirring occasionally, until the sweet potatoes are tender and the chili has thickened. Taste and add additional chili powder, if desired. We usually do 2 1/2 tablespoons. Season with additional salt, if necessary.
Serve warm with your favorite toppings, such as shredded cheese, green onions, sour cream or Greek yogurt, jalapeños, or avocado.

title: Zesty Lemon Chicken Pasta Dinner with Classic Caesar Salad & Artisan Bread
ingredients: 16 ounces Farfalle Pasta (454g), or your favorite bite-size pasta
1 head romaine lettuce, torn into bite-sized pieces
instructions: Begin by preparing the Caesar dressing: In a small bowl, combine 3 minced garlic cloves, 3/4 cup mayonnaise, 2 tablespoons grated Parmesan cheese, 5 minced anchovy fillets, 1 tablespoon lemon juice, 1 teaspoon Worcestershire sauce, and 1 teaspoon Dijon mustard. Season with salt and pepper to taste and refrigerate until ready to use.
Preheat oven to 350°F for the bread (if planning to warm it).

Prepare the croutons: Heat 1/4 cup olive oil in a large skillet over medium heat. Add 3 quartered garlic cloves and cook until brown, then remove garlic. Add 4 cups cubed day-old bread to the flavored oil and cook, turning frequently, until lightly browned. Transfer to a plate and season with salt and pepper.
Fill a large pot with water for the pasta, add salt, and bring to a boil.

While waiting for the water to boil, season 2-3 chicken breasts on both sides with salt, pepper, 1 teaspoon lemon zest, 1/2 teaspoon garlic powder, 1/2 teaspoon Italian seasoning, and 1/2 teaspoon onion powder.
Heat 2 tablespoons olive oil in a large skillet over medium-high heat. Add the seasoned chicken and cook for 3-5 minutes per side until cooked through (165°F internal temperature).
While the chicken is cooking, add 16 ounces (454g) farfalle pasta to the boiling water and cook according to package instructions until al dente.
If warming the artisan bread, place it in the preheated oven for 5 minutes for a crisp crust.
Remove the cooked chicken to a plate and tent with foil to keep warm while it rests.
In the same skillet used for chicken, add the remaining 1 tablespoon olive oil. Once hot, add 1 chopped zucchini and 1 chopped yellow squash.
Season vegetables with salt, pepper, remaining 1/2 teaspoon garlic powder and remaining 1/2 teaspoon Italian seasoning. Cook on medium-high heat for 2 minutes.
Add 3 cloves minced garlic to the vegetables and cook for 30 seconds until fragrant.
Drain the cooked pasta, reserving 1/4 cup of pasta water if needed for the sauce.
For the Caesar salad: Place 1 head of torn romaine lettuce in a large bowl.
Add the hot cooked pasta to the skillet with vegetables along with 4 tablespoons (57g) butter and 1/4 cup (60g) fresh lemon juice. Toss everything together until butter has completely melted.
Sprinkle 1 cup (95g) freshly grated Parmesan cheese over the pasta mixture and toss to combine.
Chop the rested chicken into bite-sized pieces and return to the pan with the pasta, mixing gently to incorporate.
Garnish the pasta with 1/4 cup (15g) fresh chopped parsley and additional Parmesan cheese if desired.
Back to the salad: Add the refrigerated dressing, remaining 4 tablespoons Parmesan cheese, and the prepared croutons to the romaine lettuce. Toss well to coat evenly.
Remove the artisan bread from the oven if warming, or serve at room temperature after slicing as needed.
Serve the lemon garlic chicken pasta alongside the Caesar salad and sliced artisan bread for a complete meal.

title: Zesty Lemon Chicken Pasta with Classic Caesar Side Salad
ingredients: 16 ounces Farfalle Pasta (454g), or your favorite bite-size pasta
1 head romaine lettuce, torn into bite-sized pieces
instructions: Begin by preparing the Caesar dressing (can be made ahead): In a small bowl, combine 3 minced garlic cloves, 3/4 cup mayonnaise, 2 tablespoons grated Parmesan cheese, 5 minced anchovy fillets, 1 tablespoon lemon juice, 1 teaspoon Worcestershire sauce, and 1 teaspoon Dijon mustard. Season with salt and pepper to taste and refrigerate until ready to use.
Fill a large pot with water for the pasta and bring to a boil.
While water is heating, season chicken breasts on both sides with salt, pepper, 1 teaspoon lemon zest, 1/2 teaspoon garlic powder, 1/2 teaspoon Italian seasoning, and 1/2 teaspoon onion powder.
Heat 2 tablespoons olive oil in a large skillet over medium-high heat. Add the seasoned chicken and cook for 3-5 minutes per side until cooked through (165°F internal temperature).

While chicken is cooking, prepare the croutons: Heat 1/4 cup olive oil in another skillet over medium heat. Add 3 quartered garlic cloves and cook until brown, then remove garlic. Add 4 cups cubed day-old bread to the flavored oil and cook, turning frequently, until lightly browned. Transfer to a plate and season with salt and pepper.
Once chicken is cooked, remove to a plate and tent with foil to keep warm while it rests.
Add the pasta to the boiling water and cook according to package instructions until al dente.
While pasta cooks, add remaining 1 tablespoon olive oil to the same skillet used for chicken. Once hot, add the chopped zucchini and yellow squash.
Season vegetables with salt, pepper, remaining 1/2 teaspoon garlic powder and remaining 1/2 teaspoon Italian seasoning. Cook on medium-high heat for 2 minutes.
Add 3 cloves minced garlic to the vegetables and cook for 30 seconds until fragrant.

When pasta is done, drain it and add the hot pasta to the skillet with the vegetables along with 4 tablespoons butter and 1/4 cup fresh lemon juice. Toss everything together until butter has completely melted.
Sprinkle 1 cup freshly grated Parmesan cheese over the pasta mixture and toss to combine.
Chop the rested chicken into bite-sized pieces and return to the pan, mixing gently to incorporate.
For the salad: Place 1 head of torn romaine lettuce in a large bowl. Add the prepared Caesar dressing, remaining 4 tablespoons Parmesan cheese, and the croutons. Toss well to coat evenly.
Garnish the pasta with 1/4 cup fresh chopped parsley and additional Parmesan cheese if desired.
Serve the lemon garlic chicken pasta with the Caesar salad on the side.
